instances.description: Optional prefab instances. Each entry expands into a regular entity at scene-load time. The engine validates these against the prefab registry; missing prefabs surface as AGF_SCENE_INSTANCE_PREFAB_MISSING.

environment.description: Image-based-lighting environment (M21-env-generated). When absent, the renderer applies the `generated` RoomEnvironment fallback so MeshStandardMaterial / MeshPhysicalMaterial render with believable PBR out of the box. Set `kind: "none"` to opt out (e.g. for a fully unlit project).

id.description: Entity id after expansion; must be unique in the scene.

prefab.description: Id of a prefab declared in this project's prefabs/ directory.

overrides.description: Component overrides merged on top of the prefab's components at expansion time. Keys are component names.

parent.description: Optional entity id of this transform's parent. When set, position/rotation/scale are interpreted in the parent's local frame; otherwise they are world-frame. Cycle / self-reference / missing-target are validated by engine check.

lightComponent.description: Light source (M21-light-schema). Polymorphic on `kind`. Position + rotation come from the entity's Transform — `LightSyncSystem` pushes the resolved world transform onto the underlying Three.js light. `castShadow` enables shadow-map rendering for that light (gated by the renderer's shadow pipeline, see M21-shadow-basic).

shadow.description: Per-light shadow-map config; ignored unless castShadow is true.

camera.description: Orthographic shadow camera frustum (directional lights) or near/far cutoffs (spot/point).

angle.description: Cone half-angle in radians; max π/2.

target.description: Optional entity id whose world position the spot points at. Defaults to scene origin.
Optional entity id whose world position the directional light points at. Defaults to scene origin.

shadowFlagsComponent.description: Per-mesh shadow opt-out (M21-shadow-basic). When absent, default is { cast: true, receive: true } — matching Unity's defaults. Set either to false as a perf escape hatch.

characterController3DComponent.description: M24-character: Rapier kinematic character controller. Pairs with `RigidBody3D { type: "kinematicPosition" }` + `Collider3D { kind: "capsule" }`. Gameplay code writes per-step intent through `runtime.physics.moveCharacter(entityId, [dx, dy, dz])` (or the runtime-only `CharacterIntent3D` component); CharacterMovementSystem runs the Rapier controller, applies corrected movement, writes the resolved transform + a `Grounded3D` runtime component.

offset.description: Skin distance kept between the character and other colliders (Rapier offset parameter).

maxSlopeDegrees.description: Steepest slope angle the character can climb.

snapToGroundDistance.description: Vertical distance to snap to ground after a downward correction (handles small steps). 0 disables snapping.

applyImpulsesToDynamicBodies.description: If true, the character pushes dynamic bodies it touches (instead of just sliding off them).

mass.description: Effective mass for impulse calculations when push-pushing dynamic bodies.

rigidBody3DComponent.description: M24-schema: declares an entity as a Rapier rigid body. Pair with a sibling Collider3D for the actual collision shape. 'fixed' = static level geometry. 'dynamic' = simulated, writes Transform after fixed step. 'kinematicPosition' = controlled by gameplay/character systems, participates in collisions.

type.description: Body kind. AGF_RIGIDBODY3D_DYNAMIC_TRIMESH rejects dynamic+trimesh combos at validation time.

mass.description: Mass in kg. Required for dynamic bodies; ignored for fixed bodies. Validator emits AGF_RIGIDBODY3D_MASS_REQUIRED when missing on dynamic.

gravityScale.description: 0 = floats, 1 = standard, >1 = heavier. Negative values flip gravity for this body.

lockRotations.description: Freeze all rotations — useful for upright character controllers.

ccd.description: Continuous collision detection. Costlier but prevents fast-moving bodies tunneling through thin colliders.

canSleep.description: Allow Rapier to put the body to sleep when idle.

collider3DComponent.description: M24-schema: collision shape for a sibling RigidBody3D entity. Primitives (box/sphere/capsule/cylinder), trimesh (arbitrary static geometry — must pair with RigidBody3D.type="fixed"), and heightfield (terrain). Set `sensor: true` for trigger volumes that emit events without resolving collision.

offset.description: Local-space offset relative to the body's Transform.position.

rotation.description: Local-space rotation (degrees, XYZ Euler) relative to the body's Transform.rotation.

sensor.description: Trigger volume — emits enter/exit events but doesn't push other bodies.

layer.description: Named collision layer (e.g. 'world', 'player'). Resolved against project.json#physics.layers at engine check time.

mask.description: Layers this collider interacts with. AGF_COLLIDER3D_LAYER_UNKNOWN catches unknown names.

size.description: Full extent on each axis (Rapier wants half-extents; adapter divides by 2).

halfHeight.description: Distance from center to cap-base (excluding hemispheres).

vertices.description: Flat XYZ vertex array (`[x0, y0, z0, x1, y1, z1, ...]`). Length must be a multiple of 3 and ≥ 9 (one triangle). Authored in body-local space — Rapier applies the body's pose at step time.

indices.description: Triangle index array (`[i0, i1, i2, i3, i4, i5, ...]`). Length must be a multiple of 3.

rows.description: Number of height samples along the Z axis. `heights.length` must equal `rows * columns`.

columns.description: Number of height samples along the X axis.

heights.description: Row-major Y samples (`heights[row * columns + col]`). Pre-scale values.

scale.description: World-space size of the full heightfield. Y is the vertical scale applied to `heights` entries.

followCameraComponent.description: M21-cam-follow: anchor the camera to a target entity. Each frame FollowCameraSystem reads the target's world position, places the camera at `targetPosition + offset`, and orients it toward `targetPosition + lookAtOffset`. `smoothing` blends the previous camera position toward the new target per-frame (1 = snap, 0 = frozen).

offset.description: Camera position relative to the target.

lookAtOffset.description: Optional offset added to the target before computing the look-at direction; useful for looking slightly above the head.

smoothing.description: Per-frame lerp factor toward the desired position. 1 = snap, 0 = never move.

orbitCameraComponent.description: M21-cam-orbit: pure-data orbit-camera state. Place on the same entity as `Camera` + `Transform`. OrbitCameraSystem reads `(target, distance, pitch, yaw)` each frame and writes Transform.position + rotation so the camera looks at `target` from the given polar angles. Gameplay systems (mouse drag, follow-target, etc.) mutate these fields via applyCommands; the system stays input-agnostic.

target.description: World-space point the camera looks at.

pitch.description: Down-from-horizon angle in degrees. 0 = horizontal; positive = looking down.

yaw.description: Angle around Y axis in degrees.

lodComponent.description: M17-lod: per-entity LOD chain. LodSelectionSystem reads the active camera's world position, picks the lowest-maxDistance level whose threshold the entity is inside, and writes that level's mesh / material onto MeshRenderer. Levels must be sorted by maxDistance ascending. `fallback` decides what happens past the last threshold — `last` keeps the cheapest mesh, `hide` drops the entity from rendering by removing MeshRenderer.

maxDistance.description: Distance from the active camera below which this level renders.

batchableComponent.description: Opts an entity into the M17 renderer batcher. BatchingSystem groups entities by (mesh + material + shadow flags + group) and emits one Three.js InstancedMesh per bucket. The `group` field is an authoring hint — siblings sharing the same group prefer the same bucket. The renderer treats entities WITHOUT this component as un-batched (single Mesh per entity). Omit to keep the existing per-entity path.

group.description: Optional authoring hint. Entities with the same group + matching mesh/material/shadow keys share an InstancedMesh bucket.

path.description: M17 bucketing path. `instanced` (default) groups by (mesh + colour + shadow + group) into a Three.js InstancedMesh — same geometry, varied transforms. `batched` groups by (colour + shadow + group) into a BatchedMesh — varied geometry, shared material; useful when many distinct primitive meshes share one material/colour. Mesh refs that point at GLBs are not eligible for either path yet.
